author: jih2nn
content: Can nothing go wrong even when optional proofs validators become majority at the worst case?

author: barnabasbusa
content: they gonna start getting penalized if they miss many attestations

author: barnabasbusa
content: Nature will take care of the quick recovery if this happens 😄

author: barnabasbusa
content: I also do not see any node operator handling serious amount of ETH to willing to opt in for optional proofs tbh

author: barnabasbusa
content: The cost of them missing a single attestation with 5000ETH staked already is probably less than the cost of running that EL for a month

author: jih2nn
content: Won't they attest to an invalid block as well?

author: barnabasbusa
content: if they get no proof, they won't be able to attest no?

author: jih2nn
content: They don't check the result of execution engine nor proof engine, no?

author: barnabasbusa
content: zkboost would check the validity of the proof no?

author: jih2nn
content: Could you elaborate?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I thought the proof verification is done by the zkboost process

author: jih2nn
content: It's done right after the execution engine call. See L166 in your PR

author: barnabasbusa
content: what if we gate the validator's attestation on either the local el or verified gossiped proof?

author: jih2nn
content: What do you mean by gating?

author: barnabasbusa
content: validator won't be able to attest unless they have received the gossiped proof

author: jih2nn
content: In your scenario, you still don't want to rely on its validness, right?

author: taulepton_
content: Yes that’s current how it works in lh. We import the block optimistically if no EL exists (do not mark the block as valid or invalid). We then have a hook after successful validation of k/n execution proofs that sets the block to valid and as such ready for attestation.

content: updated the pr to add description about the gating validators

author: barnabasbusa
content: <@402080271563751435> the main reason I've added the validator.md comment is because Jihoon's https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1464778559313809538/1494616752577974352 comment

author: barnabasbusa
content: I do think its implied, but I don't think we should rely purely on poeople's implied assumption 😄

author: taulepton_
content: Yep I think that's a fair statement 😅

author: barnabasbusa
content: also regarding your comment regarding whether we should be keeping the assertion or not, I think we should not. Assertion would imply that we expect a validated response imo

author: taulepton_
content: Yeah I actually think there's already a bit of ambiguity with the assertion becuase the NOT_VALIDATED state does in practice pass the assertion

author: barnabasbusa
content: I also don't think that the node should be in "optimistic" mode if the proof has been received

author: barnabasbusa
content: Oh I didn't see this one

author: taulepton_
content: yep if the proof is received and validated then the block is marked as valid

author: barnabasbusa
content: I feel like this is generally a pretty bad practice lol

author: barnabasbusa
content: "not validated"  but "valid" block?

author: taulepton_
content: Agreed I was a little confused by it. It seems that the optimistic import mechanism was initially intended as optional but in practice it has pretty deep cross deps with the rest of the specs.

author: taulepton_
content: hahahaa yep. I think this is also required though

author: taulepton_
content: As in we want to be able to import blocks optimistically. That at least allows the beacon node to know the latest proposed block even if they cant yet validate it via EL / PE. This prevents them from attesting to old blocks etc.

author: taulepton_
content: Small write up on this here https://hackmd.io/@frisitano/BJ9P2IpQ-e

author: taulepton_
content: Regarding fire and forget. I think the implication is that if we do have an EL and the EL returns valid then we should mark the block as valid immediately. If there is no EL then we should just proceed to `proof_engine.notify_new_payload`. I think the "assert" tried to imply this (but didn't do a great job of it).

author: taulepton_
content: The PR looks good to me but I would defer to <@592004585506340885> on how best to capture these nuances.

author: jih2nn
content: Nicely put. FYI, this is what I meant in the above.

author: jih2nn
content: I think optimistic import can be implementation details. The spec will dictate what we should agree on. Maybe cleaner approach could be asserting the EL in the spec but clients may give users an option to rely on the PE. That would also make it easier to merge 8025 spec into the canonical specs.

author: taulepton_
content: In practice how do you think we should capture this in the spec? Possibly adding a note to the `assert execution_engine.verify_and_notify_new_payload` to note that if the execution engine is not configured then the call should default to `NOT_VALIDATED` status?

author: jih2nn
content: Not asserting in either the EL or the PE in beacon-chain.md but stating that you should pick either of them in validator.md seems a bit contradicting to me. Perhaps there is a better way.

author: taulepton_
content: Or are you saying no changes AND no note and just leave it to imp details communicated out of band?

author: jih2nn
content: I was thinking about this without 8025. What about a client not calling the EL today? Does it put us in the same situation?

author: jih2nn
content: I would need to know what other people think in order to answer this, especially from client teams. Do all clients prefer to make the EL optional?

author: taulepton_
content: In terms of client implementations its not supported but conceptually yes if they dont call the EL then the execution payload is `NOT_VALIDATED`

author: jih2nn
content: At the end, the spec is basically what we agree on. It seems we're trying to reach to that consensus at the moment. (Or I'm just the only one who is left behind lol)

author: taulepton_
content:
Yep and I think this is largely composed of two main questions:
1) Do we want to support no EL at the CL specs layer
2) How do we effectively represent the no EL flows at the CL specs layer

author: jih2nn
content: Ah, so the consensus is not calling the EL at all? Or you can call the EL but you don't have to?

author: jih2nn
content: It's the latter I think, based on what you described above

author: taulepton_
content: Currently the specs (without EIP8025) enforce that you MUST call the EL

author: taulepton_
content: But what I mean to say is that conceptually / logically if you do not call the EL then you have no idea abou the validity of the execution payload and as such that execution payload must be considered `NOT_VALIDATED`. i.e. you would skip atttestions that are based on this block.

author: jih2nn
content: Umm.. but what if you have k+ proofs?

author: taulepton_
content: That's only relevant for EIP8025

author: taulepton_
content: In EIP8025 if you have no EL but have validated k of n proofs for a specific execution payload then you mark it as valid
